board_level_success_criteria: High-impact action classes are named before agents receive tool authority.
Any irreversible or broad-impact action has explicit human approval, identity, policy, and receipt evidence.
Runtime kill signals override prompt goals, planner state, memory state, and connector output.
Cross-agent, context, authorization, memory, and egress boundaries are checked before escalation.
Catastrophic-risk scenarios can be replayed after model, connector, prompt, policy, or context drift.

annex_controls.control_objective: Name high-impact action classes, impacted domains, approved workflows, and forbidden action paths before any agent receives MCP authority.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Which action classes are considered high impact, and where is the deny-by-default inventory?

scenarios.title: Loss of human oversight over high-impact action

annex_controls.title: High-impact action inventory

annex_controls.control_objective: Require explicit human approval, risk acceptance, and receipt linkage before high-impact action can proceed.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Can a reviewer reconstruct who approved the high-impact action and which risk acceptance applied?

scenarios.title: Loss of human oversight over high-impact action

annex_controls.title: Approval and risk-acceptance gate

annex_controls.control_objective: Kill the agent session when runtime behavior shows approval bypass, deny-after-deny looping, broader token requests, secret egress, or irreversible action without approval.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Which runtime attributes force a kill decision even if the agent says the task is still in scope?

scenarios.title: Uncontrolled autonomous loop or tool cascade

annex_controls.title: Runtime kill-signal override

annex_controls.control_objective: Prevent token passthrough, wrong-audience tokens, scope drift, shadow connector authority, and implicit user-token inheritance.
annex_controls.diligence_question: How does the platform prove an agent acted through a scoped non-human identity rather than a borrowed user token?
annex_controls.title: Token and identity containment

annex_controls.control_objective: Minimize inter-agent payloads and block hidden prompts, private memory, raw traces, credentials, and authority state from crossing boundaries.
annex_controls.diligence_question: What stops a compromised agent from delegating poisoned context or excess authority to another agent?
annex_controls.title: Cross-agent containment

annex_controls.control_objective: Kill or hold any attempt to move secrets, unredacted PII, private memory, context evidence, or trust-center data outside approved destinations.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Can context egress policy stop leakage to model providers, telemetry, webhooks, other tenants, or public corpora?
annex_controls.title: Data-boundary kill

annex_controls.control_objective: Tie severe scenarios to red-team drills, measurement probes, expected runtime decisions, and rerun triggers after model, connector, policy, or context drift.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Which catastrophic-risk cases are replayed after drift, and what decision is expected?

scenarios.title: Uncontrolled autonomous loop or tool cascade

annex_controls.title: Replayable catastrophic-risk evals

annex_controls.control_objective: Expose severe-risk controls as a buyer-readable trust-center packet with evidence paths, MCP tools, status, residual risk, and acquisition diligence answers.
annex_controls.diligence_question: Can a buyer or acquirer inspect catastrophic-risk readiness without reverse-engineering the repository?

annex_controls.title: Trust-center reporting

buyer_views.question: Does SecurityRecipes have a credible future enterprise assurance surface beyond open prompts and docs?
buyer_views.title: Acquisition diligence

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can the organization prove that high-impact agent actions stop when approval, identity, policy, or receipt evidence is missing?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Turns board-level oversight into inspectable runtime evidence rather than a policy PDF.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: High-impact automation stays in hold mode until workflow ownership, approval records, identity scope, receipt evidence, and readiness decision are present.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Loss of human oversight over high-impact action

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can the platform stop an agent that loops across tools, ignores deny decisions, or converts one task into a broader action chain?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Gives platform teams a runtime stop story for autonomous behavior that is technically working but operationally unsafe.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Expansion requires loop detection, deny-after-deny kill signals, red-team replay, and receipt reconstruction for the full action chain.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Uncontrolled autonomous loop or tool cascade

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can the buyer prove an agent cannot pass tokens across MCP servers, request broader audience tokens, or silently inherit user authority?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Directly addresses the MCP authorization and non-human identity concerns large AI platforms must solve.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Any token, scope, audience, or identity escalation without conformance evidence and approval is denied or killed.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Credential, token, or delegated-authority cascade

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can one compromised or confused agent avoid spreading hidden prompts, authority, memory, or tool traces into another agent?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Extends SecurityRecipes from MCP-safe retrieval into multi-agent safety and governance.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Inter-agent handoffs require signed identity context, minimized payload fields, approved data classes, explicit authority state, and replayable red-team cases.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Cross-agent cascading failure

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can an agent change dependencies, base images, build caches, release assets, or generated code without silently expanding fleet-wide exposure?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Makes security remediation agents credible for supply-chain work without hiding fleet-level risk.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Fleet-impacting changes require capability-risk review, scoped workflow manifests, skill provenance, readiness evidence, and receipt-linked verifier output.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Supply-chain autonomy blast radius

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can approved context, private memory, secrets, PII, or customer evidence be prevented from leaving the tenant, model, telemetry, MCP, or public-corpus boundary?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Answers the privacy and data-governance concern that blocks enterprise agent adoption.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Any secret, unredacted PII, private memory, or trust-center evidence egress without data-class and destination policy is killed.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Private-context exfiltration or public-corpus leakage

catastrophic_scenarios.board_question: Can agents be kept out of irreversible actions unless a separate human-operated approval and risk acceptance path exists?
catastrophic_scenarios.buyer_value: Creates a credible answer for boards, insurers, regulated enterprises, and acquirers reviewing high-consequence autonomy.
catastrophic_scenarios.promotion_gate: Irreversible action remains denied unless a buyer-approved architecture accepts the residual risk, records approval, and keeps the agent outside direct execution.
catastrophic_scenarios.title: Irreversible financial or critical-infrastructure action

commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, cloud provider, or security vendor gets an immediately legible severe-risk assurance layer for agentic tool use and MCP adoption.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Sell hosted high-impact action inventory, customer-specific risk acceptance, approval receipts, runtime kill policy, drift replay, and trust-center export APIs.
commercialization_path.open_layer: Publish the catastrophic-risk annex as generated open evidence so teams can inspect severe scenarios, default decisions, source packs, and MCP tools.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes exposes a testable catastrophic-risk annex for agentic AI: high-impact action classes, default decisions, kill signals, source evidence, and MCP tools are machine-readable before agents act.
default_questions_answered: Which severe scenarios are in scope?
Which actions require human approval and risk acceptance?
Which runtime signals kill a session?
Which evidence packs and MCP tools prove readiness?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this annex to board AI risk review, AI platform intake, high-impact MCP tool approval, procurement security review, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open severe-risk evidence, then sell hosted approval receipts, runtime kill policy, customer-specific risk acceptance, replay, and trust-center exports.

intent: Define a testable catastrophic-risk annex for SecurityRecipes so high-impact agentic workflows can be held, denied, or killed before autonomous behavior creates irreversible business, safety, financial, privacy, supply-chain, or critical-infrastructure consequences.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Product Security, GRC, model-risk, board risk, safety, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Catastrophic-risk control annex for agentic AI and MCP runtime decisions
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can show how high-impact autonomous actions are inventoried, evidence-bound, reviewed, stopped, replayed, and reported through generated JSON and MCP tools before agents act.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI is shifting from assistant output to delegated action across MCP servers, private context, identity providers, code hosts, deployment systems, and agent-to-agent handoffs. Enterprise buyers need a practical control annex for rare but severe failure modes, not only a list of prompt-injection mitigations.

kill_signals: agent attempts to bypass a human approval gate
agent requests a broader token, connector scope, or identity role than the workflow declares
agent loops across tools after a deny or hold decision
agent attempts to move secrets, private memory, or unredacted PII to an external destination
agent attempts funds movement, production deployment, identity administration, or mass deletion without a signed approval record

top_risk_workflows.title: Base Image and Container Layer Remediation

standards_alignment.coverage: Tracks CSAI's 2026 catastrophic-risk direction for testable controls around loss of human oversight, uncontrolled system behavior, large-scale irreversible impact, and assurance scaffolding for the agentic control plane.
standards_alignment.name: CSA STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex

standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns catastrophic-risk controls to identity, authorization, orchestration, runtime behavior, risk intelligence, certification, and trust assurance layers for autonomous AI agent ecosystems.
standards_alignment.name: CSA Securing the Agentic Control Plane

standards_alignment.coverage: Maps to NIST priorities for industry-led standards, community-led protocols, agent authentication, identity infrastructure, and security evaluations for human-agent and multi-agent interactions.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative

standards_alignment.coverage: Covers agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, supply-chain compromise, unexpected code execution, context poisoning, inter-agent failures, cascading failures, trust exploitation, and rogue-agent behavior.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026

standards_alignment.coverage: Anchors high-impact MCP action controls in OAuth 2.1, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, protected resource metadata, and token-passthrough denial.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification

standards_alignment.coverage: Supports governance, measurement, provenance, monitoring, incident response, and lifecycle risk treatment for deployed generative AI systems.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
